interval.label: Alarm Interval
interval.tooltip: Sets the alarm frequency. For example, setting it to 5 seconds means no new alarm will be generated within 5 seconds after this algorithm triggers an alarm for the camera.

length.label: Alarm Window Length
length.tooltip: The judgment period for alarm events. For example, setting it to 5 times means the system needs 5 consecutive analysis hits before deciding whether to trigger an alarm. Lower values make alarms more sensitive but may increase false alarms.

threshold.label: Alarm Threshold
threshold.tooltip: Usually used together with the alarm window length. For example, if the window length is set to 5 and the threshold is set to 3, an alarm is triggered when the algorithm detects 3 hits within the 5-count window.

conf_thres.label: Hazardous Sound Confidence
conf_thres.tooltip: Increase the confidence threshold when higher precision and fewer false alarms are required. Use a lower threshold when the scenario requires detecting as many abnormal sounds as possible.
